Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 1. **Deconstruct and Analyze the Event Log:** * I need to calculate the total resolution time for each case. * Total resolution time = Time of Close Ticket - Time of Receive Ticket. * I also need to look at individual step durations to identify bottlenecks. * *Case 101:* * Receive: 2024-03-01 08:00 * Close: 2024-03-01 10:15 * Total: 2 hours 15 minutes. * Steps: Receive->Triage (10m), Triage->Assign (10m), Assign->Investigate (40m), Investigate->Resolve (60m), Resolve->Close (15m). * Max step: Investigate (60m). No escalation. * *Case 102:* * Receive: 2024-03-01 08:05 * Close: 2024-03-02 09:15 * Total: ~25 hours 10 minutes. * Steps: Receive->Triage (25m), Triage->Assign (30m), Assign->Escalate (150m), Escalate->Investigate (150m), Investigate->Resolve (1140m), Resolve->Close (15m). * Max step: Investigate->Resolve (19 hours). Escalation present. * *Case 103:* * Receive: 2024-03-01 08:10 * Close: 2024-03-01 09:30 * Total: 1 hour 20 minutes. * Steps: Receive->Triage (5m), Triage->Assign (15m), Assign->Investigate (15m), Investigate->Resolve (30m), Resolve->Close (15m). * Max step: Triage->Assign (15m) or Assign->Investigate (15m). No escalation. Very fast. * *Case 104:* * Receive: 2024-03-01 08:20 * Close: 2024-03-02 08:30 * Total: 24 hours 10 minutes. * Steps: Receive->Triage (40m), Triage->Assign (30m), Assign->Investigate (210m = 3.5 hours), Investigate->Resolve (1110m = 18.5 hours? 19 hours), Resolve->Close (30m). * Max step: Investigate->Resolve (19 hours). No escalation. * *Case 105:* * Receive: 2024-03-01 08:25 * Close: 2024-03-03 09:30 * Total: ~49 hours 5 minutes. * Steps: Receive->Triage (10m), Triage->Assign (25m), Assign->Investigate (10m), Investigate->Escalate (150m = 2.5 hours), Escalate->Investigate (1080m = 18 hours? 24 hours), Investigate->Resolve (1020m = 17 hours? 19 hours), Resolve->Close (30m). * Max step: Escalate->Investigate (24 hours). Investigate->Resolve (19 hours). Escalation present.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 2. **Compare Total Times:** * 101: 2h 15m * 102: 25h 10m * 103: 1h 20m * 104: 24h 10m * 105: 49h 05m * Average (approx): 20.4 hours. * Median: 24h 10m. * Outliers/Long cases: 102, 104, 105. * Fast cases: 101, 103. * Conclusion: Cases 102, 104, and 105 have significantly longer resolution times.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 3. **Analyze Root Causes for Long Cases:** * *Case 102:* * Escalation to Level-2. * Big delay: Assign->Escalate (2.5h), Escalate->Investigate (2.5h), Investigate->Resolve (19h). * *Case 104:* * No escalation. * Big delay: Assign->Investigate (3.5h), Investigate->Resolve (19h). * *Case 105:* * Escalation to Level-2. * Big delay: Investigate->Escalate (2.5h), Escalate->Investigate (24h), Investigate->Resolve (19h). * *Commonalities in Long Cases:* * All long cases (102, 104, 105) have a massive delay between Investigate Issue and Resolve Ticket. * 102: 19 hours. * 104: 19 hours. * 105: 19 hours.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: * Looking at timestamps: * 102: Inv 14:00 Mon -> Res 09:00 Tue. * 104: Inv 13:00 Mon -> Res 08:00 Tue. * 105: Inv 14:00 Tue -> Res 09:00 Wed. * Pattern: Investigation happens mid-afternoon. Resolution happens next morning. * This strongly suggests a Next Business Day cycle or an overnight batch process. If the issue isnt resolved by end of day, it rolls over to the next day.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 4. **Synthesize Findings and Recommendations:** * *Significant Delays:* Cases 102, 104, 105. * *Root Causes:* * **Escalation Latency:** Escalations introduce waiting periods. Case 105 waited 24 hours for Level-2 to pick up. Case 102 waited 2.5 hours. * **Investigation-to-Resolution Overlap/Overnight Gap:** The most consistent factor is the ~19-hour gap between Investigation and Resolution in all long cases. This corresponds to a Next Day resolution. * **Complexity vs. Escalation:** Case 104 didnt escalate but still took two days. * **Wait Times:** * 104: Assign->Investigate is 3.5 hours. * 105: Escalate->Investigate is 24 hours.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 5. **Structure the Response:** * *Self-Correction/Sanity Check:* * Did I calculate Case 104 correctly? Yes. * Did I calculate Case 105 correctly? Yes. * Comparison: 101/103 are ~1-2 hours. 102/104/105 are ~25-50 hours. Clear separation. * The Investigate->Resolve gap is the smoking gun. 19 hours in all slow cases. This is likely the dominant factor. * Result: The 19h gap is the primary driver. Escalations add secondary delays.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: 6. **Final Polish (Mental Outline of Response):** * *Cycle Times:* 101: 2h 15m ... * *Root Causes:* 1. **Overnight/Next-Day Resolution Gap:** All slow cases show a ~19-hour delay ... 2. **Escalation Bottlenecks:** ... 3. **Investigation Start Delays:** ... * *Recommendations:* * Optimize afternoon workflow ... * Reduce escalation response time (SLA). ...
